findings.finding_title: SQL Injection in /api/users endpoint allows full database dump via id parameter
findings.cwe_claimed: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
findings.summary: The endpoint interpolates the id parameter directly into a SQL query and leaks data to low-privilege users.
steps_to_reproduce: 3. Observe database rows in the HTTP response.
findings.observed_result: The response body includes usernames and password hashes from the users table.
findings.impact_claimed: Any authenticated user can dump the users table and target administrative accounts.
findings.remediation_suggested: Replace string concatenation with a prepared statement in UserController.php line 142.
findings.researcher_notes: Confirmed against the local whitebox environment.

unconfirmed_candidates.summary: The endpoint performs arbitrary server-side fetches without allowlist validation.
steps_to_reproduce: 1. POST a URL parameter that targets cloud metadata.
2. Observe that the current environment cannot reach metadata endpoints.
unconfirmed_candidates.observed_result: The current test environment is not on AWS so the impact could not be confirmed.
unconfirmed_candidates.impact_claimed: A cloud deployment could expose metadata and short-lived credentials.
unconfirmed_candidates.remediation_suggested: Allowlist trusted destinations before issuing server-side requests.
unconfirmed_candidates.researcher_notes: Code path is reachable but the local environment is not representative.
unconfirmed_candidates.reason_not_confirmed: Local environment has no cloud metadata endpoint to validate impact.
